members.name: Dr. Rajendra Uprety
members.about: An inventive agriculturalist, PhD from Wageningen University with more than 30 years of experience both domestically and abroad. Possess real-world expertise and experiences to support the development of context-specific agricultural technologies. successfully manage and organize a variety of initiatives, projects, and programs. A good team builder appreciates group projects and teamwork and is constantly innovative.

members.name: MAGELYN G. GOZE, PhD
members.about: She is a graduate of Bachelor in Secondary Education, Master of Science in Teaching and Doctor of Philosophy of Education at saint Paul University Philippines. She is an author and a researcher, who published two academics books; an instructor and dissertation adviser of Saint Paul University Philippines; a module writer and curriculum validator of Department of Education, Philippines.

members.name: Mr. Shirdad Mohammadi
members.about: Mr. Shirdad Mohammadi is a banking and financial sector professional with over 10 years banking experience in various positions in Risk Management, Lending and grants operations. He has been working with The First Microfinance Bank – Afghanistan in different positions such as Risk Management Officer, Acting Credit Risk Analyst and Loan Officer. He has also worked as a Senior Lending Expert with OXUS Afghanistan an affiliate of ACTED which is a leading financial service provider in the country of Afghanistan. Currently, shirdad is working in the position of Credit Analyst with one of the reputed private institutions which support the MSMEs both in Urban and Rural areas. He holds a master’s degree in Economic Science from the university Faiz Mohammad Kateb and Bachelor degree in Business Administration with specialization in Finance and Banking with various certificates in M&E, Rural Project Management, Risk Management, Accounting, Leadership and So on. He has also served for over 5 years as a volunteer in the Education Board of Aga Khan National Council for Afghanistan.

members.name: Mr. Gnanamuthu Bosco Rajah
members.about: I am a development sector worker with more than 13 years experience with international development organisataions. I have masters in Sociology, Bachelors in Management, Diploma in Sociology, Diploma in Transformational Development, Short Diploma in Community Nutrition, Certificate in SDGs, Certificate in M& E, Certificate in Research for Impact . I am a member of United Nations Volunteers (UNV), International Consortium for Social Development(ICSD), International Sociological Association as well (ISA).

members.name: Mr. Tshering Wangchuk
members.about: Founder and President of the Horticulture Association of Bhutan, Mr. Tshering Wangchuk is a stalwart in the realm of horticulture and international exhibitions. With a diverse educational background and extensive leadership roles across various associations and events, Mr. Wangchuk has made significant contributions to the horticultural industry, both in Bhutan and globally. Educational Background: Bachelor of Arts in English and Dzongkha, Royal Thimphu College. Professional and Leadership Positions: · President, Horticulture Association of Bhutan. · Executive Committee Member, Bhutan Chamber of Commerce & Industry. · Executive Committee Member, BCCI Committee of Sector Associations. Bhutan Commissioner General at various international exhibitions including: · International Horticultural Expo 2023, Doha, Qatar. · International Horticultural Exhibition 2024, Chengdu. · Expo 2019, Beijing. · 2018臺中世界花卉博覽會 Taichung World Flora Exposition. · Ambassador, Yunnan Commercial Representative Office in Bhutan. · Steering Committee Member, South Asian Rainwater Harvesting Network. · Country Representative, International Association for Agricultural Sustainability. · Country Representative, International Association of Horticultural Producers – AIPH. · Former President, Triennale Milano. · Former President, FloraliesInternationales – France. · President, Bureau Multinational Expositions. Achievements: · Runner Up in Landscape Design at the Taichung Expo in Taiwan, 2018 · Recipient of the prestigious Dr. APJ Abdul Kalam International Award, 2023

members.name: Mr. Onkareshwar Pandey
members.about: Mr. Onkareshwar Pandey is an accomplished thought leader with over 30 years of multifunctional and multi-sectoral experience. He serves as a Member of the Expert Panel for selecting Senior Fellows under CCRT, Ministry of Education, Government of India, and is an Executive Fellow at Woxsen University. He has worked extensively across media and knowledge platforms, including print, television, digital media, TV news agencies, and think tanks. Widely regarded as an institution builder, he has a strong track record of creating impactful brands and nurturing leadership. A veteran journalist, Mr. Pandey has held key leadership roles such as Group Editor, Managing Editor, Media Head, and CEO across major English and Hindi media organizations. His notable positions include Senior Group Editor at Rashtriya Sahara, Managing Editor of The Sunday Indian (published in 14 languages), Editor at ANI (India’s largest TV news agency), and Opening Anchor at Sahara TV. Currently, he is the CEO and Editor-in-Chief of Observer Global Media Group, New Delhi. He is also an author and editor of 10 books and has traveled to over 10 countries. Mr. Pandey serves on the boards of several institutions that provide strategic,360-degree solutions for brand development, networking, and thought leadership. He is the Founder and CEO of Golden Signatures Research & Consulting, a techno-innovative think-and-do tank that has established 11 knowledge networks connecting over 3, 500 thought leaders across India and more than 40 Commonwealth countries. Through initiatives such as the Edu Thought Leaders Forum and the Commonwealth Thought Leaders Forum, he has brought together vice-chancellors, academicians, diplomats, bureaucrats, and global experts. Over the past three years, he has organized more than 30 conferences, including 10 offline events. Golden Signatures collaborates with institutions such as NITI AAYOG, NIDM, KDEM, MCA, state governments, central universities, and reputed NGOs to promote knowledge leadership. Notable keynote speakers at his events include Mr. Amitabh Kant, Mr. Kabir Bedi, Ms. Jaya Prada, and Ms. Dipika Chikhalia. He has launched several prestigious national and international awards and is himself a recipient of honors such as the Atal Bihari Vajpayee Award for Journalism, Lok Nayak Jai Prakash International Award, and Best Journalist of the Year (2011), among others. Mr. Pandey is actively associated with several social organizations, including Broadcasters Club of India (Executive President), Dignity India (Executive Director), Diaspora India (Managing Trustee), and ADRID (Executive Director), which focuses on disaster management. He is also a Google Certified Trainer on debunking fake news in media and holds a diploma in Yoga from Vishwayatan Yoga Kendra, Katra, J&K. He continues to organize knowledge events in collaboration with corporate leaders and publications such as Business Connect Magazine.

members.name: Amb. Dr. Smily Mukta Ghosal
members.about: An Indo-American plant biologist and environmentalist from Dallas, Texas, USA, she worked for over 12 years as a corporate business trainer before reconnecting with her childhood passion as a nature lover and launching her own green beauty line, “#1Naturalbeauty,” offering customized skin and hair products. She is highly self-motivated, ambitious, and driven, with a continuous mission to encourage people worldwide to Go Green and adopt natural products, promoting a sustainable lifestyle away from synthetics. A dynamic leader with extensive experience as an intuitive wellness educator, environmentalist, herbalist, international motivational speaker, transformational coach, certified mentor, pageantry trainer, personality groomer, soft skills and leadership trainer, and women empowerment advocate. In addition to being recognized among the world’s highest achievers and receiving more than 600 awards, she has served as a keynote speaker at international summits and conclaves focused on education, wellness, green entrepreneurship, women empowerment, and positive influence. Some of her notable recognitions include “Excellence Global Green Champion,” “Humanitarian & Peace Queen,” “Mother of the Environment,” and inclusion in the Rekordbreakers News Prestigious Hall of Fame 2023. Her ongoing work focuses on bringing nature back into modern lifestyles and inspiring people to protect Mother Earth by adopting green practices, planting trees, and promoting sustainability. She strongly believes that nature provides everything needed for a healthy life, eliminating the need for synthetic products. With a strong foundation in Ayurvedic heritage, she has built a unique brand focused on serving humanity while empowering others. She believes in leading by example, maintaining authenticity, and continuously uplifting and supporting communities. Her key green initiatives include: Global Green Revolution Campaign, Women Empowerment Pageant, Global Women Greenpreneurs Club, Green Modelling and Fashion University, Environmental Awareness & Sustainability Campaigns, Sustainable Lifestyle Workstation, Natural Healing Temple & Tourism Center. Highlights of her awards and recognitions include: Green Initiative Leader, Global Green Queen, Outstanding Leadership Award, Global Influencer, Golden Legend Award, Mother Teresa Peace Award, recognition from the Kingdom of West Timor (Indonesia) and the Royal Kingdom of Ghana, Top 100 Inspirational Women, Global Glory Icon, Best Green Initiative Leader, Women’s World Record holder, International Women Icon, Global Peace Award, and many more. Her global positions and titles include Honorary Doctorate in Social Work, Humanitarian Law, Women Empowerment & Green Leadership; International Advisory Board Member (Mental Care, Govt. of India); Global Chair – IGC (UK); Advisory Board Member – Asian-African Chamber of Commerce and Industry (AACCI); President – Rotaract Asia (under Rotary International); International Board Member – International Women Empowerment Forum; Special Envoy – Humanitarian Services (African Diaspora Union); Africa Real Estate Ambassador. She is also featured in multiple world record platforms including Influencer Book of Records, World’s Greatest Record, OMG Book of Records, World Book of Records London, State Book of World Records, Netaji World Records, Exclusive World Records, Transoceana World Records (USA), and Amazing Book of World Record. Core areas of expertise include Consulting, Mentoring, Coaching, Branding, Influencing, and Promotion and Advertisement.

members.name: Dr. Nageswara Rao Ambati
members.role: Head of GFSRD-Global Centre for Social Enterprise and Civil Society
members.about: An experienced academic leader with a strong background in managing and successfully completing multiple research projects, demonstrating excellence in organization and interdisciplinary collaboration. Recognized for effective leadership, the individual was promoted to Dean, Research and Publication, where they supervised twenty research centers conducting activities in social, management, and legal domains. Their role emphasized integrating research with teaching, promoting research-based academic initiatives, and enhancing university research performance, reflected in improved rankings at GNLU NIRF and state levels between 2020–2022. They have extensive experience engaging with funding agencies, including national and international organizations, governments, think tanks, civil society organizations, and the judiciary, and have successfully proposed and established Endowment Chairs and Fellowships, managing six such chairs under their supervision. These include GNLU-Microsoft Chair on IPR Law and Policy Research, GNLU-GUJCOST Research Centre of Excellence on IPR Laws, GNLU-DPIIT Chair on IPR Research, GNLU-Nalinchandra Desai Law Foundation Chair, GNLU-SJVN Fellowship in Hydro Power Development and Environment Laws, and GNLU-GUVNL Research Fellowship on Energy Law and Policy. Additionally, they have conducted numerous Faculty Development Programs (FDPs), research methodology workshops, and conferences supported by national institutions, introduced PG Diploma programs in IPR and Biotechnology, and facilitated multiple international collaborations through MOUs with foreign institutions, contributing significantly to research advancement and academic growth.

members.name: Dr. Sujeewa Polgampala
members.role: Head of GFSRD- Global Centre for Education and Skill Development
members.about: Dr. Sujeewa Polgampala is a dedicated and visionary education professional with 29 years of experience in the field of education. She currently serves as Vice President (Academic & Quality Assurance) at a reputed teacher education college in Sri Lanka, where she has successfully managed and overseen academic programs, curriculum development initiatives, and student services. Her work involves close collaboration with faculty, staff, parents, and community stakeholders to ensure the delivery of high-quality education that meets diverse student needs. She has a proven track record of providing strategic direction and fostering positive learning environments, having developed and implemented comprehensive strategic plans aligned with institutional goals, resulting in improved academic performance and increased student engagement. She possesses strong expertise in designing and implementing innovative curriculum frameworks that integrate modern teaching methodologies and technology. Additionally, she has led curriculum committees and facilitated professional development programs for educators to enhance instructional practices and promote student-centered learning. Known for her strong communication and leadership skills, she effectively builds relationships with diverse stakeholders, facilitates productive collaboration, and promotes inclusive and supportive educational environments.

members.name: Tapas Ranjan Chakraborty
members.about: Tapas Ranjan Chakraborty has been working in climate change and natural resource management for the last 24 years, focusing on Community Resilience, Technology Transfer, Biodiversity Conservation and Ecosystem Management. He worked with BRAC, Oxfam, Bangladesh POUSH, CNRS, BCAS and SDRC.   He has developed a number of training and awareness materials on Climate Action and Natural Resource management. He is with different networks and groups for the management of natural resources and Nature-based Solutions. He was the first country coordinator of the Asia Youth Environment Network supported by the Commonwealth Youth Programme. He was one of the initiators that started ‘Know Risk No Risk’ movement in Bangladesh. He is skilled in policy influence. His areas of interest are action research on climate change, nature-based Solutions and popularizing citizen science. He has developed more than 70 mass awareness materials on climate change and has a good number of research works published on climate action and natural resource management. Bridging the gap between science and policy is his passion.

members.name: Muhammad Sharifur Rahman
members.about: I am Mohammad Sharifur Rahman, Graduated from Calcutta University, India. Chairman – SDG Smart Village Bangladesh. Professionally a Consultant of SDGs Action Research at the International Research Center, SIMEC Institute of Technology, Uttara, Dhaka-1230. Program Coordinator Institute of SDG Studies, International University of Bangladesh Agriculture and Technology-IUBAT. Bangladesh Country Head, Sustainable Procurement and Supply Chain-SPSC Scotland-UK. CEO, Jago Uddokta Ltd. My hobby: Social Research, Capacity Building Training, Presentation, Public Speaking.

members.name: Maria Christina Endarwati
members.about: Maria Christina Endarwati is one of the professional planners in Indonesia, starting her career in the world of urban and regional planning since 1998 to form a sharp analysis in the field of urban and regional planning. Studying at the National Institute of Technology Malang under the direction of experienced lecturers in the world of planners made her a reliable planner, so in 2000 she decided to take a master’s degree to deepen her knowledge of regional and urban planning at RMIT Australia. After returning to study from Australia, he immediately devoted himself to his beloved campus, Institut Teknologi Nasional Malang. Over time with the achievements made Maria Christina Endarwati was appointed as a Civil Servant by the Batu City government to become the personal secretary of the mayor of Batu. Feeling that it was enough to devote herself to Batu City in the following year decided to resign and refocus on the world of spatial planning in Indonesia and became a permanent lecturer at the National Institute of Technology Malang. Over time with a myriad of experiences as a planner practitioner lecturer in Indonesia led him to become one of the assessors for the Indonesian Planner Expert Association. In addition, he is a member of the Indonesian Planner School Association and is often a speaker at events organized by GFRSD (Global Forum For Sustainability Rural Development). Returning to the world of education, she continued her doctoral program at the faculty of environmental science at Brawaijaya University with a focus on the world of hatred and received her doctorate in 2022 cum laude. And most recently Maria Christina joined the National Capital City development project.

members.name: Dr. Maria Teresa Pedrosa Silva Clerici
Prof. Amir Mohammad Nasrullah
members.role: Head, GFSRD-Global Centre for Local Government
members.about: Prof. Amir Mohammad Nasrullah, a Professor of Public Administration, University of Chittagong, Bangladesh received his Doctor of Philosophy (Ph.D.) degree in Management Studies from Brunel Business School, Brunel University London, The UK. Earlier he received his Master of Social Science (MSS) degree with Honors in Public Administration from the University of Chittagong, Bangladesh. Professor Nasrullah regularly teaches various courses on the broader field of governance, public administration and management including issues and theories of Public Administration, Rural Development, Local Government, Public Policy, Environmental Management, Entrepreneurship Development, Public and Non-Profit Management, Project Management, Total Quality Management, Organizational Behavior, and Human Resource Management, etc. Professor Amir Nasrullah has published five books, including Rethinking Development in South Asia: Issues, Perspective and Practices, (Co-edited), published by Cambridge Scholars Publishing, London, The UK. He presented many research papers at different national and international conferences. Consequently, he has contributed more than 40 scholarly articles and book chapters published by different publishers, such as Cambridge Scholars, Routledge, McMillan, Serials, UPL etc. Dr. Amir’s current research work is on “Fighting with the New Normal Post COVID 19 World: A Study on the Governance Challenges in Bangladesh” under the Chittagong University Research and Publication Cell. His recently finished research work (Worked as a Team member) is “Assessing Risks in the City of Chittagong, Bangladesh using the Climate and Ocean Risk Vulnerability Index (CORVI)” in collaboration with Independent University, Bangladesh, the Ocean Policy Research Institute (OPRI) of the Sasakawa Peace Foundation, Tokyo, Japan, and the Stimson Center, Washington DC, USA. Dr. Amir is associated with many national and international organizations such as the British Academy of Management (BAM), London; International Council for Small Business (ICSB), George Washington University, USA; South Asian Network for Governance Studies (SANGS); Bangladesh British Alumni Network (BBAN); South Asian Network for Public Administration (SANPA); The Asiatic Society of Bangladesh; Centre for Environment and Sustainability (CES), Salam Islam Foundation (SIF) etc. He is also a trustee of Bandarban University; member of Board of Advanced Studies, Chittagong University; member of Academic Council of Southern University, Bandarban University, and Britania University etc. Professor Amir Nasrullah regularly supervises M.Phil. and Ph.D. research and serves as an external examiner of many universities at the Ph.D. level. He also serves as a guest faculty member at different universities in Bangladesh, as well as, a trainer at many government centres such as Public Administration Training Centre (PATC), Bangladesh Avademy for Rural Development (BARD), Bangladesh Institute of Administration and Management (BIAM), Engineering Academy, and Bankers Club Chittagong etc.

members.name: Prof. Prodyut Bhattacharya
members.role: Head, GFSRD-Global Centre for Bamboo and other NTFP Promotion
members.about: Prof. Prodyut Bhattacharya is Dean of University School of Environment Management at GGS Indraprastha University, Govt. of NCT of Delhi, New Delhi. He is Professor with 27 years work experience in the area of Natural Resource Management, and performs basic job responsibility on teaching, research, consulting and academic administration. He has been actively involved in publication, research, training and teaching for post graduate and PhD level. A fellow of Beahrs Environmental Leadership Program (ELP) of the University of California, Berkley, USA and Visiting Scientist fellowship (JSPS) under University of Tsukuba, Japan. Received advance training on community forestry, and microfinance & Livelihood from RECOFTC, Kassetsart University, Bangkok; University of Naropa, Colorado, USA respectively. He was the founder Coordinator to set up the International Centre for community Forestry (ICCF) at IIFM, Bhopal. Prof. Bhattacharya has 130 publications in international and national journals including articles, technical reports, conference papers and co-authored five books. He has completed 10 International sponsored research projects (ITTO, IIED, DFID, IUCN, JICA, FAO, IDRC etc), & 14 other nationally sponsored research projects from multilateral/bilateral donors Government, Govt. of India and Corporate bodies. Guided 12 Ph.D. scholars, some are pursuing and supervisor of 26 M.Phil theses, and 75 master level dissertations. Completed 19 consultancy assignments for Government, donor organization and for industries, and widely travelled to more than 20 countries with different academic assignments. His research interest pertain to the broad NRM issues and Livelihood linkages; in subject area of Biodiversity Conservation, Forest Ecology, Sustainable NTFP Management, effect of climate change, Ecotourism, Urban-Greenspace, Forest Governance, Certification and Sustainable Forest Management. Having keen interest and passion for the nature based work, and networking.

members.name: Dr. Helen R. Sekar
members.role: Head, GFSRD-Global Resource Centre for Ensuring Childhood
members.about: Dr. Helen R. Sekar holding her M. Phil and PhD from the Madras Christian College, Madras University. Presently the Senior most Faculty Member holding the position of Senior Fellow at the V.V. Giri National Labour Institute (VVGNLI), NOIDA. She is coordinator of the National Resource Centre on Child Labour and the Editor of the quarterly ‘Child Hope’. Involved in primary and secondary research, advocacy, training, planning, monitoring and evaluation over three decades mostly focusing on children’s economic and social roles, health and educational aspects and labour dimensions of migrant and trafficked children, legislation and enforcement, impact of technological change on the demand of child labour, structures, etc. Conceptualized the geographical area-specific strategy and methodology of organizing the unorganized labour and conducted a series of rural camps. Presented research papers in different national and international forums and published. Developed curriculum for different Universities in India. Examiner for many Ph. D and M. Phil scholars in Central and State Universities across the country. Coordinated the SAARC-level conferences and international-level Training Courses. Associated as a Member of major National Level Commissions, Committees, Boards, Working Groups, etc., constituted by the Government of India and ccollaborating with with the Central Ministries and State Governments and other social partners and stakeholders ccontributing to policy formulation and implementation

members.name: Prof. U N Roy
members.role: Head, GFSRD-Global Centre for Rural Technology
members.about: Prof. U N Roy, working as Professor, Rural Development at National Institute of Technical Teachers Training and Research, Chandigarh. I did my post graduation in Rural Development (XISS, Ranchi) as MSc Environmental Science (Nalanda:Open University, Patna) and PhD in Sociology from Panjab University, Chandigarh on Integrated Watershed Management. I got UGC, Government of India, Junior Research Fellowship in Rural Development in 1992 and joined my PhD initially at CDRT, IIT Delhi. I was there as UGC research fellow for two years working on Sustainable Agriculture Project but shifted my PhD work at PU, Chandigarh as I joined NITTTR Chandigarh as Lecturer in 1995. Since 1995, he is working as faculty member at NITTTR Chandigarh. His area of research and interest are Technologies for Sustainable Rural Development, Organic farming and sustainable agriculture, watershed management, Climate change and disaster management, participatory research (PRA/RRA). He had also been working for vocational training of rural youth and women under Community Polytechnic Scheme of GoI through a network of 155 polytechnics in Northern India training about 70 – 80 thousand unemployed youth and women per annum from poorer sections of the society. Dr. Roy established a Weekly Organic farmers market at Chandigarh in 2015 which has been doing well benefiting about 30 organic farmers of HP, Haryana, Punjab and Chandigarh states.

members.name: Mr. Tulsi Ram Pandey
members.role: Head, GFSRD-Global Centre for Youth Development
members.about: Mr. Tulsi Ram Pandey is a Strategic Analyst, Management Consultant, Environmentalist, Academician, International Resource person/ Speaker, research and capacity-building specialist and trainer with expertise in research, training and development to maximize human resource outputs in sustainable development. He is a proven advisor to the different national and international organizations , and has been playing an active role in leading , providing guidance on research and development functions to promote and innovative solutions to social developmental challenges. He is an influential and inspirational leader with excellent human capital development skills. He has conducted professional development workshops and capacity building sessions in national and international level. He has been serving as a leader, Representer, advisor and consultant for organizations including CG Institute of Management, Access to Human Right International ( AHRI) , Global Environmental and Climate conservation initiative, International internship University , Climate Action Group , International social science CEOs congress, International youth Development council, world Leaders summit, International social science and Management welfare association Nepal, The policy times, world youth United Nation, Beyond book Publication, Namaste India Group International, Professional Institute of Business Bangladesh, Lions Club of Knowledge Galaxy. He has received the Honorary Doctorate from the International Diplomatic organization for the development and peace. He has been pursuing his PHD in Transformational Leadership and sustainable Development. He has done Mphil, MBA and MA in economics degree. He has published numbers of articles in international journal and simultaneously has been working as an editor in international journals.

members.name: Dr. Om Katel
members.role: Head, GFSRD-Global Centre for Environment and Climate Change

members.name: Prof. Ala Uddin
members.role: Head, GFSRD-Global Centre for Indigenous People
members.about: Prof. Dr. Ala Uddin teaches anthropology in the Department of Anthropology, University of Chittagong, Bangladesh. I work across a number of related topics that might be summed up as ways of approaching unity and diversity. I conduct research on issues of the indigenous peoples in the Chittagong Hill Tracts, Bangladesh. My major research dealt with the survival strategies of these indigenous peoples affected by outsiders (i.e., settler Bengalis) encroaching on their land. Among other issues, I worked on: religious pluralism, street vending, health and food, migration and diaspora, the refugee situation, overseas migration of women workers, and the informal economy. I have co-authored a book entitled Theoretical Anthropology (in Bengali), and authored several articles published in peer-reviewed national and international journals of repute. I am currently researching the Rohingya refugee crisis and the Covid-19 situation in Bangladesh. I was a Fulbright scholar in the Department of Religious Studies, University of California, Santa Barbara (UCSB) on “Religious Pluralism and Public Presence,” in 2014.

members.name: Dr. Abhishek Das
members.role: Head, GFSRD - Global Centre for ICT and Rural Infrastructure
members.about: Dr. Abhishek Das is currently working as an Associate Professor and former Head in the Dept. of Computer Sc. & Engineering at Aliah University, Kolkata. His Research Area is in Medical Image Processing, Machine Learning, Data Science and IoT. He has received a Post Doctoral Research position from University of West Scotland, UK by a Fellowship received from the European Commission. He has also worked as an Assistant Professor in the Dept. of Information Technology, Tripura University (A Central University), India. He has worked previously as a Reader in Computer Sc. & Engineering, Indian Institute of Space Science & Technology(IIST) and Lecturer in Information Technology, Bengal Engineering and Science University, Shibpur (now IIEST Shibpur). He is a Guest Editor of Microsystem Technologies (SCI Indexed Journal), Associate Editor of S.I. in Indonesian Journal of Electrical Engineering and Computer (Scopus Indexed Journal), Editor of S.I. in IAES International Journal of Artificial Intelligence, (Scopus Indexed journal). He also has filed 7 patents in his name as co-inventor at India Patent Office, Govt. of India. His Ph.D is from the Dept. of Computer Sc. & Engineering, Jadavpur University, India. His M.S. in Electrical & Computer Engineering from Kansas State University, USA and B.Tech in Computer Science & Engineering from Kalyani University, India. He has worked as a Business System Analyst in Blue Cross Blue Shield New York, USA & as a Quality Analyst in Vensoft Inc. Phoenix, USA and also as a Consultant Project Manager in the Software Industry. He is also a Visiting Scientist at Indian Statistical Institute, Kolkata. He has also worked in Technical Educational Administration as Regional Officer and Asst. Director (on deputation) at AICTE(under MHRD, Govt. of India). He is an NCERT National Scholar, New Delhi and a Tilford Dow Scholar, USA. His Biography has been published in the 28th edition of National Dean’s List, USA. He is a Fellow of IETE, Fellow of NBSP ( Regd. under Ministry of Corporate Affairs, Govt. of India), Fellow of South Asian Chamber of Scientific Research & Development(SAIARD) (certified by MSME, Govt. of India) , Member of IEEE, Computer Society of India, ACM and an Honorary Senior Member of IACSIT Singapore. He has several publications in International and National Journals and peer-reviewed Conferences. He is also a TPC Committee Member of IEEE and other international conferences in Europe and South-East Asia.

members.name: Dr. Amrita Sarkar
members.role: Head, GFSRD-Global Centre for Public Health Care and Management
members.about: Dr. Amrita Sarkar is a doctor and public health specialist by profession. I am affiliated to the Department of Community Medicine, Tomo Riba Institute of Health And Medical Sciences (TRIHMS) under the state government. I had done MBBS, MD (Community Medicine). CPH (Manchester), CAMBRA (California), IHPE (Michigan) and am a FAIMER fellow, interested in Inter professional research in approaching to various health issues in a holistic way. Her main interest is women & child health and I have served as the Former State Maternal health Consultant (Additional) for Assam and published a book on the same. Please share with people who may find it useful Breastfeeding: The Art and Science of Lactation.

members.name: Mr. Venuste NIYIRORA
members.role: Head, GFSRD-Global Centre for Rural Entrepreneurship and Livelihood
members.about: Mr. Venuste NIYIRORA is, Co-Founder and the current CEO at EPIGA Services Rwanda, young Rwandan entrepreneur passionate about entrepreneurship and business world and relies on a network of contacts in the area on the national and international level, all of whom are happy to take part in different development projects. Over the past years, Vénuste has gained experience working as a secondary school teacher (after graduation from Kigali Institute of Education in 2011) and trainer for different private institutions and non-governmental organizations (both national and international). He has built a solid reputation in this field in Rwanda for more than eight (8) years and has been awarded by the Ministry of Education through the Rwanda Education Board as the best performing teacher at Southern Province level. He possesses sound knowledge about financial management, investment analysis, entrepreneurship, education and economics concepts and prevalent industry information thereby providing reliable decision support. He has strong academic credentials and holds a Master of Financial Management awarded by Amity University Uttar Pradesh, India in 2016. He also holds two bachelor’s degrees namely the Bachelor of Financial and Investment Analysis from the said university and the Bachelor of Social Sciences (Hons), Economics with Education and QTS (Secondary) from Kigali Institute of Education. He represented both the working institutions and the Country in different national and international training related to entrepreneurship and promotion of the income generating activities and improving youth and women livelihoods especially in rural areas. He received different awards and certificates out of which were awarded as a result of better performance in different domains. He is a life member of World Peace Development& Research Foundation. He is also one of the founding members of the Global Forum for Sustainable Rural Development (GFSRD) and a member of the YALI (Young African Leaders Initiative) Network. Venuste visited and traveled to different countries including those of Western and Eastern Africa and Asia where he lectured in different universities and institutions including National Institute of Entrepreneurship and Small Business Development (NIESBUD, India), Manipal University Jaipur, India, International Chamber of Media and Entertainment Industry, NOIDA, India, and National University of Rwanda among others. With his experience, know-how, and contacts, Vénuste will make your experience in rural entrepreneurship memorable, while also benefiting the society at large especially the youth and women as much as possible.

members.name: Dr. A. K. Singh
members.role: Head, GFSRD-Global Centre for Rural-Urban Linkages Studies
members.about: Dr. A. K. Singh did his M. A. (Special) in Political Science, Lucknow University, Lucknow in 1984, was awarded Ph. D. In Political Science by Lucknow University, Lucknow in 1991. He is working as Assistant Director, Regional Centre for Urban and Environmental Studies, a nodal resource centre of Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs, Government of India, Lucknow. He has about 30 years of experience in teaching, research and training in the field of social science, management, and behavioural sciences. He has organised and conducted scores of seminars, workshops and training programmes on contemporary issues at national, regional and state levels. He was Member, Working Group on Child Rights for Twelfth Plan, Planning Commission, Government of India, New Delhi in 2012. Presently Dr. Singh is Editor of Indian Journal of Development Research and Social Action, International Journal of Rural Development and Management Studies, Journal of Asian Business Management, International Journal of Human Development and Information System, and International Journal of Management Research and Technology, being published by Global Research Publications, New Delhi, International Science Press, Gurgaon, and Serials Publications, New Delhi. He has guided several research scholars and students in pursuing of their doctoral and post doctoral research. He has contributed as Consultant and Project Director in various projects and programmes funded by DFID, UNICEF, World Bank, UNDP, UNESCO, UNAIDS and various Ministries and Departments of Central as well State Governments. He is a prolific writer and has to his credit an array of published books, scholarly papers, and reports. He was awarded Gold Medal for Best Research Paper in the national seminar in 1999. He received Outstanding Professional Award by American Biographical Institute, California, USA, in 2001, 500 Leaders of Influence, ABI, USA in 2000, and was felicitated by Bharat Jyoti Award and Certificate of Excellence as well as Rajiv Gandhi National Excellence Award by India International Friendship Society, Best Citizen of India Award by International Publishing House, New Delhi, in 2012, Bhojpuri Samman, 2014, U. P. Ratan Award in 2015 , Outstanding Scientist Award in 2016 and Glory of India Award in 2017. Dr. Singh was felicitated with Life Time Achievement Award by A J. Group of Institutions, Mangalore, Karnataka in 2017 and Dr. S. N. Ghatak Memorial Life Time Achievement Award by Life Foundation and Sahyog Swavlamban Samiti, Lucknow in 2019. He was felicitated by Distinguished Scientist award by VDGOOD Professional Association, Trichy , Tamil Nadu in 2020. Dr. Singh is associated with a number of academic institutions, Corporate Houses, reputed Government and Non-government organizations.

members.name: Dr. Sridhar Seetharaman
members.role: Head, GFSRD-Global Centre for Gender Equality
members.about: Dr. Sridhar Seetharaman is currently Advisor (Gender and Rural Development), Arupa Mission Research Foundation, Bhubaneswar, Odisha. He has done considerable research on Entrepreneurial Development, Quality Management, Gender Justice, Gender Budget and Gender Audit. He was the Gender Budget Consultant to the Ministry of Women and Child Development, Government of India. As the Consultant he assisted the Government of Nagaland in Engendering their State Plan. He has organised several training programmes on Gender aspects in India and abroad. He has handled sessions on Gender Concerns in Development and Gender Budget and Audit at the Administrative Staff College of India, Hyderabad, Telangana, India and the Fiscal Policy Institute, Bengaluru, Karnataka, India and at various State and National level Institutes. He is a Visiting Professor for Development Planning Models at the Jawaharlal Nehru Architecture and Fine Arts University, Hyderabad, Telangana, India and Professor Emeritus for Gender Studies at the Jadavpur University, Kolkata, West Bengal, India and at United Theological College, Bengaluru, Karnataka, India. He is the recipient of the Best Citizen of India Award, 2014 and the Champion of Children’s Award 2014 and member of the European Institute for Gender Equality, Vilnius, Lithuania since 2017. He was invited by the UNO to be the Leader for their Project on Gender Integration in Mainstream Development of the Canadian Economy in 2019.

members.name: Dr. Mohinder Slariya
members.role: Head, GFSRD-Global Centre for Inclusive Development
members.about: Dr. Mohinder Slariya is an Environmental Sociologist by expertise and Sociologist by education and training, working for Department of Higher Education, Govt. of Himachal Pradesh India and presently posted at Govt. College, Chamba, Himachal Pradesh, India. He has been post-doc fellow of Finnish Science Academy. My major research interests are; human dimensions of development, environment, Climate change, food security, tribal studies. Dr. Salaria is passionate researcher and believe in training youth for next generation to serve the society in better way with the help of objective and scientific understanding. So far, I have contributed in different capacities in national and international conferences, symposiums, seminars and workshop in different countries.

members.name: Dr. Kanchan Bhowmik
members.role: Head, GFSRD-Global Centre for Sustainable Agriculture & Food Security
members.about: Dr. Kanchan Kumar Bhowmik completed his Honours’ Graduate & Master Degree in Agriculture from Bidhan Chandra Krishi Viswavidyalaya (B.C.K.V) and his research leading to Ph.D on fertilizer management at Rural Development Centre, IIT, Kharagpur. He received National Award (Rank 88 in West Bengal in West Bengal Board of Secondary Education), All India Science Talent Search Award, Achievement-cum-Diagnostic Awards in Mathematics as well as National Fellowship from I.C.A.R. He was also awarded by the then Governor, Honble Sri Viren J. Shah, Govt. of W.B; Honble Sri Somnath Chatterjee, the then Speaker of Loksava for his remarkable works. He was also selected for Research Work at Massey University, New Zealand. He published several research papers in national and international journals and written many scientific articles in different books and periodicals. He served as Asst. Director in Loka Kalyan Parishad (Panchayat Raj Research Centre) for two years. Presently he is working as State Project Manager (Livelihoods) under West Bengal Rural Livelihoods Mission, Govt. of W.B after long term service as an Agriculture Officer at West Bengal Comprehensive Area Development Corporation, Govt. of W.B for extension of different agricultural technologies to rural communities for upliftment of the rural economy. He is also associated with many rural development Institutions i.e R.K. Mission, CAPART, MANAGE, ICAR, WBSCVE &T, FOSET, NIRD, SIPRD, BOPT under MORD, Govt. of India. He is the pioneer to introduce SRI, SWI, SMI (Maize & Mustard), TPS & ZERO BUDGETED CULTIVATION and T & D pig rearing in Rural Development Sectors of W.B.

members.name: Smt. Meenakshi Datta Ghosh, IAS (Rtd.)
members.about: Meenakshi Datta Ghosh, an independent, multidisciplinary, development professional, specializes at the interface of governance, public health and public policy. Across the social sector (Health, Population, Rural Local Government and Panchayati Raj, Urban Housing and Municipal Governance, Tourism, Industry, Grievance Redressal, Conservation for Nature, and Independent Evaluation), Ms. Ghosh has addressed multiple aspects of Government to Citizen [GtoC] services, as in policy formulation, program development, implementation, appraisal, and evaluation. Ms. Ghosh, a career bureaucrat, has held inter alia, several positions in Government of India and across Union Territory administrations, indicated below. Ms. Ghosh has worked with WHO, UNFPA, UNICEF, UNDP, IPPF (International Planned Parenthood Federation), ESCAP, the Ford Foundation, the Clinton Foundation, the Bill and Melinda Gates Foundation, as well as the Global Fund to Fight AIDS, TB and Malaria, focusing on HIV/AIDS, maternal, and neo-natal health, livelihoods and poverty alleviation. Ms. Ghosh has been Consultant with FAO Bangkok; UNDP, Manila and with Asian Development Bank (External Reviewer, `ADB Strategy to combat HIV/AIDS in the Asia Pacific Region).

members.name: Dr. Shiladitya Chatterjee, IAS (Rtd.)
members.about: I am an international development economist with expertise on India and Asia and the Pacific. Educated at Presidency College, Calcutta (BA); Delhi School of Economics (MA); and Boston University (Ph.D), I have worked in academics; administration (in the Indian Administrative Service); and international development (at the Asian Development Bank). I taught economics at St. Stephen’s College, Delhi University; Boston University; and University of Massachusetts. In India, I worked on grassroots level development in Assam; at the Central Government in the Finance Ministry (Plan Finance), and at the Prime Minister’s Office I helped PM coordinate the economic reforms of 1991-94. As an ADB economist I worked on structural reforms, fiscal decentralization, capital markets development, transport and basic infrastructure. Since 2004, as Head of ADB’s Poverty Unit, I focused increasingly on poverty reduction; forged the ADB-UNDP-ESCAP regional partnership on the MDGs; and supported formulation of the new SDGs. I have contributed to research with a large number of publications and reports. Currently I am advising UN-ESCAP on regional cooperation for the SDGs; the Government of Assam on implementing the SDGs; and the Bengal Chamber of Commerce and Industry in crafting a Vision Bengal 2030. My interests also include writing fiction and have recently published a novel.

members.name: Dr. Jayanta Choudhury
members.about: Dr. Jayanta Choudhury has wide experience in teaching and research in Rural Development for the last 22 yrs. Passionate, relentless, dedicated and true with his vision firmly focused on the horizon of Localising Sustainable Development Goal. Dr. Choudhury presently working as Assistant Professor, Department of Rural Studies, Tripura University, India. He was Associate Professor (on Deputation), National Institute of Rural Development &Panchayati Raj, North East Regional Centre, Ministry of Rural Development, Government of India for three years. He is the author of 25 books and more than 55 research articles published in National and International journals and in edited volumes. Six Ph.D. Scholars awared under his supervision. He has led/worked with different research projects in India and Bangladesh funded by World Bank, ILO, UNICEF, IGDC, GTZ, MoTA, MoRD, etc. He is also the Founder of the North East Training, Research and Advocacy (NETRA) Foundation and the Global Forum for Sustainable Rural Development (GFSRD) – an orgnisation having 6 continetal offices and 129 country chapters. . He is recognized as Expert, Inclusive Policy Lab, UNESCO. He is an active member of various Civil Society Organisations (CSOs) like IFRTD, London; Global Forum for Rural Advisory Services (GFRAS), Switzerland; Community of Evaluators, SriLanka. He is also a board member of Forum for India Development Cooperation (FIDC), Research and Information System for Developing Countries, New Delhi; Financial Inclusion Trust (2009-2019), North Eastern Financial Inclusion Trust (2019 onward) (under the aegis of Bandhan Bank, India), Bandhan Financial Holding Company, West Bengal. Global Peace Let’s Talk, London, etc. He did his Ph.D. and Masters in Rural Development from Visva-Bharati University, West Bengal. He teaches Inclusive Rural development; Rural Development Planning & Project Management; Financial Inclusion; Social Enterprises to PG students. He serves as Associate Editor for International Journal of Rural Development & Management Studies (IJRDMS) and TUI – A journal on Tribal Life and Culture. He has led various internationally funded development projects in India and Bangladesh. Received Global Award (Best Professor in Rural Development) from World Education Congress-2015 and Broad Outlook Learner Teacher (BOLT) Award-2006 from AIR INDIA, Mumbai.
